title: Pungent Peppery Korean Pork Skewers
description: A citrusy and adventurous Korean dish featuring rapeseed oil and a mortar-pestle-pound technique, these skewers are perfect for those who love spicy and snappy flavors. The combination of garlic, ginger, and Korean chili flakes creates a truly unique taste experience.
instructions: In a large mortar, pound the garlic and ginger until well combined and fragrant.
Add the Korean chili flakes, brown sugar, soy sauce, and black pepper to the mortar and pound until a smooth paste forms.
In a large bowl, whisk together the rapeseed oil and the paste from the mortar.
Add the sliced pork to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the marinade.
Thread the marinated pork onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat and cook the skewers for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with sliced green onions and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds.
Let the skewers rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
Serve with a side of kimchi or pickled vegetables for a truly authentic Korean experience.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
